items.title: Football club DNA quiz: Which team is being described?
items.summary: You'll see five real quotes depicting a team's DNA, way or philosophy. Can you guess the club each quote describes?

items.title: Birmingham troeft Ajax af in strijd om Carlos Vicente
items.summary: Ajax loopt de komst van Carlos Vicente mis, zo lijkt het. De Spaanse vleugelaanvaller gaat namelijk kiezen voor Birmingham City. Dat bevestigen bronnen rondom de speler.

items.title: 'Liverpool blokkeert transfer Robertson naar Tottenham'
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: FC Utrecht moet naar beneden kijken: 'Genoeg kwaliteit om uit deze crisis te komen'
items.summary: In crisissferen werkt FC Utrecht toe naar belangrijke weken. Na de thuisnederlaag tegen Sparta Rotterdam (0-1) lijkt Europees voetbal – toch de doelstelling in de Domstad – heel ver weg. Sterker, de ploeg van Ron Jans moet met de huidige prestaties eerder naar beneden kijken. Lichtpuntje bij FC Utrecht kan wellicht de extra aanvallende kracht van Jesper Karlsson zijn.

items.title: Ziyech maakt na half jaar rentree als ouderwetse spelverdeler
items.summary: Hakim Ziyech heeft zondagavond zijn eerste minuten gemaakt voor het Marokkaanse Wydad AC, waar hij sinds oktober onder contract staat. De ex-speler van clubs als SC Heerenveen, FC Twente en Ajax werkte de laatste maanden aan zijn fitheid en speelde bijna tachtig minuten bij zijn debuut.

items.title: Wintertransfers Eredivisie seizoen 2025/26: alle clubs op een rij
items.summary: De transferperiode is weer losgebarsten. In dit overzicht van VI vind je alle afgeronde, door clubs bevestigde, wintertransfers van de achttien Eredivisie-clubs. Clubs in Nederland kunnen tot en met 3 februari spelers aantrekken .

items.title: Cowley: I'm energised by Colchester's future - and potential buyers should be too
items.summary: When Danny Cowley took over on January 4 2024, Colchester were only five points above the Sky Bet League Two relegation zone. In the end, the U's survived by just a three-point margin.

items.title: Defending champion Sinner into Australian Open quarter-finals despite late wobble
items.summary: Defending champion Jannik Sinner made it through to his ninth consecutive Grand Slam quarter-final with a straight-sets victory over fellow Italian Luciano Darderi to stay on course for a third consecutive title in Melbourne.

items.title: Live on Sky: Norwich vs Coventry
Football transfer rumours: Trent Alexander-Arnold to join Manchester City?
items.summary: Today’s rumours are fixing a hole The thing about the transfer market that a few people forget is that not every deal comes off. Andy Robertson’s move to Tottenham from Liverpool, a head scratcher for many, seems to be OFF. It appears that Liverpool have pulled the plug over a lack of defensive options, with Saturday’s loss at Bournemouth helping them come to that decision. A long-term option, this summer, to the left-back conundrum, is said to be Antonee Robinson of Fulham. Another factor in Liverpool’s slide this season, though some fans may only admit this through gritted teeth, was the departure of Trent Alexander-Arnold. To compound the misery on that score, there’s talk that Manchester City, without a senior right-back, might attempt to loan “Trent” from Real Madrid. Or even buy him this summer. That’s a move that might set the Mersey to boiling over. Continue reading...

items.title: 'The magic has returned' - Neville 'blown away' by Carrick impact at Man Utd
items.summary: Gary Neville is "blown away" by Manchester United's transformation under the management of Michael Carrick.

items.title: Ref Watch LIVE! Praise for referee Hallam - but it was a Mosquera handball, says Bothroyd
Premier League: 10 talking points from the weekend’s action
items.summary: Casemiro is thriving under Michael Carrick, Newcastle look short of ideas and Sean Dyche takes aims at … towels? Casemiro will depart Manchester United this summer. His four years in English football have been mixed but he may yet go out on a high. At one point in his first season, such as his performance in the 2023 League Cup final, he was hailed as the club’s best signing since Eric Cantona. He never lived up to that billing, the accusation that United had overpaid for someone who left his legs in Madrid. At the Emirates in 2026, just as against Manchester City the previous week, he showed his muscle memory endures. Kobbie Mainoo is a project player for Michael Carrick. Mainoo can learn much in his remaining months alongside Casemiro, who completed the 90 minutes at Arsenal and retained his influence. United are linked with younger midfielders in Carlos Baleba, Adam Wharton and Elliot Anderson. They may now have something to live up to. John Brewin Match report: Arsenal 2-3 Manchester United Match report: Newcastle 0-2 Aston Villa Match report: Burnley 2-2 Tottenham Match report: Manchester City 2-0 Wolves Continue reading...

items.title: Michele Kang’s largesse for women’s football leaves Fifa open to bias claims | Tom Garry
items.summary: Multi-club owner’s Women’s Champions Cup sponsorship creates a conflict of interest, whatever her motivation You can imagine the meltdown across social media, if Stan Kroenke, Todd Boehly or the Glazer family were to enter into a partnership with the Football Association. Well, women’s football in the United States already took a similar unusual step in November 2024 when US Soccer announced “a historic gift” of $30m (£22m) from Michele Kang, the owner of one of the country’s biggest women’s clubs, Washington Spirit, over a five-year period. US Soccer labelled the donation as philanthropic – the largest women’s football in the country had ever had – and “non-profit”. Then, in December 2025, US Soccer unveiled the Kang Women’s Institute, a platform “designed to accelerate advancements in the women’s game through science, innovation, and elevated best practices”, and there was surprisingly little public condemnation. Overwhelmingly, the women’s game around the world appeared to celebrate the businesswoman’s generosity rather than questioning this arrangement, because of Kang’s repeatedly stated aim of trying to grow women’s football. Continue reading...

items.title: Martinez edging closer to Villa career crossroads
items.summary: Emiliano Martinez is hailed as the world's best goalkeeper at Aston Villa home games - but even though his team are riding high, the Argentina international is at a crossroads.

items.title: Beating Arsenal puts Man United into Premier League title race
items.summary: Man United are 12 points behind league-leading Arsenal, but after beating the Gunners on Sunday, can the Red Devils pull off an unlikely title push?

items.title: Arteta challenges 'poor' Arsenal after Man Utd loss
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has challenged Arsenal to show the mental strength to win the Premier League after falling to a nervous 3-2 home defeat against Manchester United.

items.title: Oviedo coach: Yamal goal from 'another galaxy'
items.summary: Real Oviedo coach Guillermo Almada branded Lamine Yamal a player from "another galaxy" after the Barcelona forward's wonder goal in Sunday's LaLiga meeting between the two sides.

items.title: Juve's Spalletti sees McKennie as 'perfect' striker
items.summary: Juventus coach Luciano Spalletti advocated for United States midfielder Weston McKennie to play in a more offensive position after his team beat Napoli 3-0 on Sunday.

items.title: Carrick cool after Arsenal win: Big games coming
items.summary: Michael Carrick said Manchester United have "bigger games to come" after a 3-2 victory against Premier League leaders Arsenal.
